articles.title: Heywood Students Visit First Choice Homes Oldham as Part of Summer School
articles.excerpt: Three teenagers from the Heywood and Middleton North constituency participated in a two-day visit to First Choice Homes Oldham, gaining insights into the housing association's operations and community support.
articles.content_html:
Three teenagers from the Heywood and Middleton North constituency spent two days at First Choice Homes Oldham as part of MP Elsie Blundell's Fly High Summer School.

Emaan Hassan, Caryn-Ann Sarpong-Kumankomah and Charlie Wilcock, all aged 16, were mentored by chief executive Mark Gifford and the senior leadership team. They engaged with various departments, including development, neighbourhood services, sustainability, and the customer contact centre.

First Choice Homes Oldham donated £300 to the Fly High Summer School to support the group's visit to the Houses of Parliament in London. Reflecting on the experience, Emaan Hassan noted the size of the organisation and the number of employees involved, while Caryn-Ann Sarpong-Kumankomah highlighted the importance of understanding how to differentiate oneself in future applications. Charlie Wilcock expressed surprise at the scale of social housing operations and the number of communities served by the organisation.

Mark Gifford stated that the organisation was pleased to host the students and emphasised the importance of such initiatives in helping young people understand democracy. MP Elsie Blundell thanked First Choice Homes Oldham for their support and sponsorship, which facilitated the visit to Parliament. She described the summer school as a means to build confidence and provide opportunities for young people.
articles.legal_disclaimer: This article was compiled from identified public sources and may be updated when further verified information becomes available.

articles.title: Meeting Requested to Discuss Hopwood Triangle Road Closure
articles.excerpt: A meeting has been requested with the Highways team and local councillors to address the impact of the Hopwood Triangle road closure in Heywood.
articles.content_html:
A meeting has been requested with the Highways team, including all councillors in Heywood, to discuss the Hopwood Triangle scheme. The request aims to address the significant impact the associated road closure has had on the town.

The closure has raised concerns among local residents and businesses, prompting the need for a collaborative discussion on the matter. The meeting is expected to focus on the implications of the road closure and potential solutions.

Details regarding the date and time of the meeting have not yet been confirmed. However, it is anticipated that the discussions will involve various stakeholders affected by the changes in traffic flow.

Further updates will be provided as more information becomes available regarding the meeting and any decisions made thereafter.

articles.title: Traffic Disruption in Heywood Due to Middleton Road Changes
articles.excerpt: Traffic in Heywood has been severely disrupted today, partly due to the ongoing pedestrianisation of Middleton Road, affecting routes throughout the town.
articles.content_html:
Traffic in Heywood has been severely disrupted today, partly due to the ongoing pedestrianisation of Middleton Road. Reports indicate that the changes are causing significant congestion across various routes in the town.

The alterations to Middleton Road are part of an experimental scheme aimed at improving pedestrian access. However, some residents have questioned the timing of the changes, particularly as other roadworks are also taking place in the area.

Witnesses described the situation as chaotic, with delays affecting not only the immediate vicinity of the 'Hopwood Triangle' but also extending throughout Heywood. The combination of roadworks and the new pedestrianisation measures has led to frustration among drivers and pedestrians alike.

Authorities have not yet commented on the situation or provided information regarding potential adjustments to the traffic management plan.

articles.title: Rochdale Youth Service Hosts Annual Youth Pride in the Park Event
articles.excerpt: Rochdale Youth Service's annual Youth Pride in the Park event attracted over 250 young attendees at Queen’s Park, Heywood, last week, featuring music, games, and activities.
articles.content_html:
Rochdale Youth Service held its annual Youth Pride in the Park event at Queen’s Park, Heywood, last week. The event saw more than 250 young people in attendance, enjoying a range of activities.

Participants enjoyed music, games, and various other activities throughout the evening, which was marked by pleasant weather. The event provided a vibrant atmosphere for attendees to engage with one another.

The event aims to celebrate diversity and promote inclusivity among young people in the community. It serves as an important platform for fostering connections and support among attendees.

This gathering allowed young people to connect and engage in a supportive environment, reinforcing the values of acceptance and community spirit.

articles.title: Roadworks on Pilsworth Road in Heywood to Begin on 17 August 2026
articles.excerpt: Pilsworth Road in Heywood will undergo roadworks starting from 17 August 2026, as announced by Rochdale Borough Council.
articles.content_html:
Pilsworth Road in Heywood will undergo roadworks starting from 17 August 2026, according to Rochdale Borough Council.

The council has not provided specific details regarding the duration of the works or any expected disruptions to traffic.

Residents and motorists are advised to plan their journeys accordingly, as the roadworks may affect local traffic patterns.

Further updates will be issued by the council as the start date approaches.

articles.title: Twelve Green Spaces in Heywood Celebrate Green Flag Award Success
articles.excerpt: Twelve green spaces across Heywood, Rochdale, Middleton and the Pennines have received the Green Flag Award for another year, recognising the efforts of local teams and volunteers.
articles.content_html:
Twelve green spaces across Heywood, Rochdale, Middleton and the Pennines are celebrating another year of success in the Green Flag Award scheme. This accolade highlights the quality and management of these green spaces, which are vital to the local community.

The Green Flag Award is a national standard for parks and green spaces, recognising well-managed sites that meet specific criteria. The award is a testament to the hard work of local teams and volunteers who dedicate their time to maintaining these areas throughout the year.

The success of these green spaces contributes positively to the local environment and community well-being, providing residents with accessible areas for recreation and relaxation.

This achievement reflects the ongoing commitment to enhancing the quality of life in the region, and it is hoped that the recognition will encourage further community involvement in maintaining and improving local green spaces.
